skill.name: context-fundamentals
skill.description: Learn how context works in agent systems, including prompts, tools, documents, history, and tool outputs.
skill.summary: Explains context components, constraints, and practical design patterns for agent systems.
skill.author: Asmayaseen

security_audit.summary: The many static external command and weak cryptography alerts are false positives from markdown code fences, prose, and non-security MD5 duplicate detection. No prompt injection, network access, command execution, or malicious intent was found. The Python helper can read caller-supplied local file paths, so publication should disclose filesystem access.

medium_findings.title: Caller-Controlled Local File Reads
medium_findings.description: The progressive disclosure helper stores a base directory but reads summary_path and detail_path directly. This supports the skill purpose, but an unsafe caller could load unintended local files.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file read behavior is direct and confirmed, but the code is a utility with no network exfiltration path. Legitimate context loading is the intended use.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The flagged external command locations are markdown examples and fenced documentation blocks. I found no shell execution call or instruction to run untrusted commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are documentation fences and illustrative prompt examples. They do not execute commands or define executable shell behavior.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are Non-Security Uses
low_findings.description: The only confirmed MD5 use hashes the first 1000 characters of section content for duplicate detection. It is not used for passwords, signatures, tokens, or integrity protection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The semantic context shows MD5 is used as a local duplicate-content key. Collision resistance is not a security boundary in this implementation.

dangerous_patterns.title: Unrestricted File Path Loading
dangerous_patterns.description: The helper reads paths supplied by reference dictionaries without enforcing the stored base_dir or checking allowed directories.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is present and may expose local files if called with unsafe paths. Risk is limited because there is no automatic caller, network sink, or malicious instruction.

content.value_statement: Agent teams often waste tokens or lose behavior because context is poorly structured. This skill explains practical context components and budgeting patterns.
actual_capabilities: Explains the main parts of agent context, including prompts, tools, documents, history, and outputs.
Describes context window limits, attention budget concerns, and context degradation risks.
Provides guidance for progressive disclosure and just-in-time document loading.
Shows how to organize system prompts with clear sections and output expectations.
Includes a Python helper for estimating tokens, truncating messages, and loading context references.
limitations: Token counting uses rough estimates and does not replace provider tokenizers.
The skill teaches concepts and patterns, not a complete production framework.
The filesystem helper needs caller-side path controls before sensitive deployments.
Examples are generic and must be adapted to each agent platform.

use_cases.title: Design a New Agent
use_cases.description: Plan which information belongs in system prompts, tool definitions, retrieved documents, and runtime history.

use_cases.title: Debug Context Failures
use_cases.description: Analyze whether missing instructions, excessive history, or noisy tool output caused an agent behavior issue.

use_cases.title: Teach Context Basics
use_cases.description: Give team members a shared vocabulary for context windows, progressive disclosure, and token budgets.

prompt_templates.title: Explain Context Components
prompt_templates.prompt: Explain the main components of agent context for my team. Use simple examples for prompts, tools, documents, history, and outputs.

prompt_templates.title: Review My Agent Context
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this agent context design and identify missing, duplicated, or low-value context. Focus on context components and attention budget risks.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Progressive Disclosure
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a progressive disclosure plan for this agent. Specify what should load at startup and what should load only when needed.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Context Budget
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a context budget for a multi-step agent workflow. Include system prompt, tools, documents, history, outputs, and compaction triggers.

output_examples.input: Review an agent that loads all product documentation at startup.
output_examples.output: The review identifies excessive startup context, recommends summary-first loading, and reserves detailed documents for task-specific retrieval.

output_examples.input: Create guidance for keeping long support conversations useful.
output_examples.output: The result proposes summary injection, recent-message retention, and concise tool outputs to preserve important context.

output_examples.input: Explain why a tool-using agent ignores a policy after many steps.
output_examples.output: The explanation connects the failure to attention budget limits and recommends placing critical policy text in stable, high-priority context.

best_practices: Use the skill before designing prompts, retrieval, or tool definitions for a new agent.
Pair the conceptual guidance with real token measurements from the target model provider.
Restrict local file paths when using the helper in environments with sensitive files.
anti_patterns: Loading all available documentation into context before the task is known.
Treating larger context windows as a replacement for careful context selection.
Using the file loading helper with untrusted paths or unrestricted directories.

faq.question: Does this skill run commands?
faq.answer: No command execution was found. The static command alerts came from markdown examples and code fences.

faq.question: Does it include executable code?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes a Python helper for context estimation, truncation, validation, and reference loading.

faq.question: Is the MD5 usage dangerous?
faq.answer: The MD5 use is for duplicate detection, not security. It should not be reused for passwords, signatures, or integrity checks.

faq.question: What tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What is the main security warning?
faq.answer: The helper can read caller-supplied local paths. Use directory allowlists before using it with sensitive files.

faq.question: Who benefits most from this skill?
faq.answer: Agent developers, prompt engineers, and technical leads who design or review context-heavy AI workflows.
